---
title: "001 — Descriptive baseline"
date: 2026-06-11
---

## Question

What does India's PV market look like at state grain — size distribution,
growth, fuel mix, seasonality, and OEM concentration? This baseline anchors
every later experiment.

## Setup

```{python}
import duckdb
import plotly.express as px
from complexity_lab.config import settings
from complexity_lab.analysis import descriptive, distributions

con = duckdb.connect(str(settings.db_path), read_only=True)
year_panel = con.execute("SELECT * FROM panel_state_year").df()
states = year_panel[year_panel.state_code != "ALL"]
LATEST_FULL = int(states.year.max()) - 1  # last year is partial
```

## Market size and growth

```{python}
all_india = year_panel[year_panel.state_code == "ALL"].set_index("year")
px.bar(all_india.reset_index(), x="year", y="total_regs",
       title="All-India PV registrations by calendar year")
```

```{python}
summary = descriptive.summary_table(states)
summary.head(12).style.format({
    "total_regs": "{:,.0f}", "cagr_5y": "{:.1%}", "ev_share": "{:.1%}",
    "cng_share": "{:.1%}", "hhi_oem": "{:,.0f}"})
```

## Size distribution: how unequal are states?

```{python}
sizes = states[states.year == LATEST_FULL].set_index("state_code")["total_regs"]
zipf = distributions.zipf_exponent(sizes)
gini = distributions.gini(sizes)
rs = distributions.rank_size(sizes)
fig = px.scatter(rs, x="log_rank", y="log_size",
                 title=f"Rank–size, {LATEST_FULL} — slope {zipf['slope']:.2f} (R²={zipf['r2']:.2f}), Gini {gini:.2f}")
fig.show()
```

A slope much steeper than −1 means registrations concentrate in top states
faster than Zipf's law would imply — consistent with car markets tracking
income and urbanization, not just population.

## Seasonality

```{python}
month_panel = con.execute(
    "SELECT * FROM panel_state_month WHERE state_code = 'ALL' ORDER BY year, month").df()
prof = descriptive.seasonality_profile(month_panel[month_panel.year.between(2013, LATEST_FULL)])
px.bar(prof.reset_index(), x="month", y="seasonal_index", error_y="std",
       title="All-India month-of-year index (1.0 = average month)")
```

## OEM concentration over time

```{python}
edges = con.execute("SELECT * FROM oem_state_edges").df()
conc = descriptive.concentration_series(edges, "year", "maker").reset_index()
px.line(conc[conc.year <= LATEST_FULL], x="year", y="hhi", markers=True,
        title="National OEM HHI (10000 = monopoly)")
```

## Finding

The market roughly doubled from ~2.4M (2013) to ~4.4M (2025) with a visible
COVID trough in 2020. State sizes are strongly unequal (Gini ≈ 0.6; rank–size
slope ≈ −1.9, steeper than Zipf). Registrations peak around the festive months
with a March fiscal-year-end bump. OEM concentration (HHI ≈ 2200) has been
declining slowly as Maruti's share erodes toward SUV-heavy challengers —
the structural question experiment 003 picks up at network grain.
